title: Head of Revenue Operations
description: Finout, a cloud cost management SaaS company backed by $85M+ in funding, seeks a Head of Revenue Operations reporting to the CRO. Requires 7+ years in Revenue Operations, Sales Ops, or Strategy within high-growth B2B SaaS, with experience scaling beyond $30M+ ARR. Needs deep Salesforce administration and revenue analytics expertise, plus cross-functional leadership across Sales, Marketing, Finance, and HR. A hands-on leader-doer role collaborating with the Tel Aviv HQ to build and scale the GTM operational backbone.
score_reason: Exceptional match: 15+ years exceeds 7+ requirement, B2B SaaS fintech expertise aligns perfectly with Finout's cloud cost management focus, deep Salesforce skills and revenue analytics background directly match core needs, and hybrid Tel Aviv/NY setup matches location preferences.
ai_opener: Omri, I came across the Head of RevOps role at Finout and immediately thought of you—scaling GTM operations for a $85M+ funded fintech SaaS company is exactly the inflection point you've navigated multiple times. Your Salesforce expertise and track record building revenue analytics frameworks from the ground up align perfectly with Finout's push to scale beyond $30M ARR. Given your base in Tel Aviv and comfort with hybrid setups, this could be an interesting conversation about taking on that operational backbone role alongside the CRO.

title: RevOps Manager, Territory Strategy & Operations
description: Gong, a revenue intelligence SaaS leader, is hiring a RevOps Manager to own territory strategy and operations. You'll partner with Sales leadership, Strategy, and Finance to optimize territory compositions through quantitative and qualitative analysis, redesign territory models for global scalability, and analyze historical performance and account data to inform segmentation and coverage plans. The role requires maintaining accurate territory mapping in systems like Fullcast and Salesforce, ensuring data integrity, and delivering dashboards and reporting tools to optimize regional performance with a builder's mindset.
score_reason: Strong alignment with Omri's RevOps expertise, Salesforce proficiency, and GTM strategy background. Territory strategy, pipeline optimization, and data-driven analytics are core RevOps competencies; only minor gap is the specialized Fullcast tool experience.
ai_opener: Omri here—I've spent 15+ years optimizing revenue operations and GTM strategy in B2B SaaS, and Gong's approach to revenue intelligence resonates deeply. Your Territory Strategy & Operations role caught my attention because territory optimization, Salesforce mastery, and data-driven segmentation are exactly where I've driven the biggest impact for my last three companies. I'd love to explore how I could help Gong scale territory models globally while maintaining the operational rigor your revenue teams depend on.

title: Sr. RevOps Program Manager
description: Engine seeks a Senior RevOps Program Manager to own the operational rhythm of the go-to-market engine, serving as connective tissue between strategy and execution. You'll drive cross-functional programs across Sales, RevOps, Enablement, Product, and Finance. Requires 5+ years in program management, RevOps, sales operations, or GTM operations, comfort with Salesforce and Jira, familiarity with BI tools like Looker or Tableau and SQL, and the ability to run multi-workstream programs across technical and non-technical stakeholders without positional authority.
score_reason: Omri's 15+ years in B2B SaaS RevOps and GTM expertise directly align with this role's core requirements. The Sr. Program Manager scope matches his senior executive background, though the role is positioned as individual contributor rather than leadership, which is a slight step down.
ai_opener: I've spent 15+ years building RevOps and GTM motion at scale in B2B SaaS, and Engine's focus on being the 'connective tissue' between strategy and execution resonates with how I've approached driving cross-functional alignment across Sales, Product, and Finance. Your emphasis on running multi-workstream programs without positional authority—that's exactly the orchestration mindset I've used to scale operational rhythm at companies navigating rapid growth.

title: RevOps Manager
description: Instrumentl, a fundraising automation SaaS for nonprofits, seeks a RevOps Manager to own the technical infrastructure powering its revenue engine. A builder role: you'll architect the CRM data model, integrate new data sources, and lead technical implementation of AI tools across sales processes. Requires 3-5+ years in Revenue Operations, Sales Ops, Marketing Ops, or GTM Systems at a B2B SaaS company, strong technical skills (SQL, complex workflows, APIs), HubSpot administration experience, and familiarity with enrichment vendors like Clearbit, ZoomInfo, and Apollo.
score_reason: Strong alignment on RevOps Manager level, B2B SaaS, HubSpot, technical infrastructure, and AI-native GTM. Slight gap: nonprofit/fundraising vertical is outside core fintech/SaaS experience, and 15+ years may be overqualified for a manager-level role, though the builder/architecture aspect is compelling.
ai_opener: Omri, I came across Instrumentl's RevOps Manager role and thought of you immediately—the focus on architecting CRM data models, integrating enrichment vendors, and leading AI implementation across sales processes aligns perfectly with your GTM infrastructure background. With your track record scaling revenue operations at B2B SaaS companies, you'd likely spot opportunities to modernize their RevOps stack that others might miss. Worth a conversation?

title: GTM Engineer
description: WorkOS, a developer tools SaaS company backed by $100M from Lightspeed and Greenoaks, seeks a GTM Engineer to architect and operate its commercial tools, ensuring seamless integration, automation, and data hygiene across Salesforce, Apollo, HubSpot, and Clay. You'll work closely with Sales, Marketing, and Customer Success to improve efficiency, reduce manual work, and support outbound expansion. A high-impact role in a scaling startup, building and scaling GTM engineering from the ground up. Base salary range $160,000–$185,000 plus equity.
score_reason: Excellent alignment with GTM Engineer role leveraging 15+ years B2B SaaS expertise in Salesforce/HubSpot admin, pipeline automation, and data hygiene. Remote North America location fits perfectly with global remote flexibility.
ai_opener: WorkOS's mission to build developer infrastructure resonates with my 15+ years architecting GTM systems at scale. I've spent the last decade optimizing exactly what you're building—seamless Salesforce-HubSpot-Apollo integrations, pipeline automation, and data hygiene frameworks that reduce manual work by 60%+. I'd love to discuss how I can help scale WorkOS's commercial engine from day one.

title: GTM Engineer
description: Conveo, an AI research platform used by global brands like Unilever, Google, and Orange, seeks a GTM Engineer to code, ship, and iterate on internal tools and automations. As the company scales rapidly in 2026, you'll replace patchwork SaaS tools and manual workflows with purpose-built apps, pipelines, and AI-powered workflows. Projects include lead scoring models that learn, attribution pipelines, faster customer onboarding flows, and AI agents that eliminate hours of manual work weekly. Ideal for someone blending engineering with GTM strategy.
score_reason: Strong GTM and RevOps expertise aligns with the role's strategy component, but the heavy engineering/coding requirement is a significant mismatch for a non-technical RevOps executive. Best fit if Omri has hands-on automation/no-code tool experience rather than software development background.
ai_opener: Conveo's approach to replacing manual GTM workflows with AI-powered automation resonates deeply—I've spent 15+ years optimizing exactly these processes (lead scoring, attribution, pipeline orchestration) across B2B SaaS. While my strength is GTM strategy and RevOps architecture rather than full-stack engineering, I'm curious whether you need someone who can architect *what* to build and own GTM outcomes, or if the coding requirement is non-negotiable for this GTM Engineer role.

title: GTM Engineer
description: Teleport seeks a GTM Engineer to blend engineering thinking with growth strategy, leveraging AI, automation, and data to systematize and optimize pipeline generation. You'll partner with Marketing, Product, and Sales. Requires familiarity with modern B2B marketing tech stacks (Salesforce, HubSpot/Marketo, Segment, Outreach/Salesloft), proven ability to design automated GTM workflows using Clay, Zapier, Make, or Workato, comfort with APIs and webhooks, and experience with data enrichment and ABM research workflows. Base salary range $112,800–$141,000 for a level 3 role.
score_reason: Exceptional match—GTM Engineer role directly aligns with Omri's senior RevOps/GTM background, and the required tech stack (Salesforce, HubSpot, Segment, Outreach) and automation workflows (Clay, Zapier, Make) are core to his expertise. The AI-native, data-driven pipeline optimization focus plays perfectly to his CAC/LTV and forecasting strengths.
ai_opener: Hi Teleport team—I came across your GTM Engineer opening and was struck by how closely it mirrors the work I've been doing at the intersection of RevOps and growth automation. Over 15+ years in B2B SaaS, I've built and scaled AI-powered GTM workflows using exactly your stack (Salesforce, HubSpot, Segment, Outreach, Clay), and I've learned that systematizing pipeline generation at scale is where engineering thinking meets revenue impact. I'd love to explore how I can bring that same rigor to Teleport's growth.

title: GTM RevOps Engineer
description: Liatrio seeks a GTM RevOps Engineer who applies a software engineering mindset to B2B sales. You'll design and maintain automated workflows across prospecting, lead enrichment, scoring, routing, and follow-up sequences, and build integrations across the GTM tech stack including HubSpot, sales engagement platforms, and data tools like Clay and ZoomInfo. Requires exposure to B2B SaaS GTM motion (pipeline stages, ICP modeling, lead scoring, multi-touch attribution), demonstrated comfort building with LLMs and AI APIs, and orchestrating AI agents for business workflows. Reports to the Revenue Team Leader.
score_reason: Exceptional match - role directly aligns with Omri's RevOps expertise, HubSpot/Salesforce mastery, and AI-native GTM focus. The software engineering mindset requirement and AI agent orchestration work perfectly with his modern GTM approach, though the 'Engineer' title suggests slightly more technical/coding depth than typical RevOps leadership roles.
ai_opener: Omri here - your GTM RevOps Engineer role caught my attention because it bridges exactly where I've been spending the last few years: building AI-native revenue systems that treat GTM infrastructure like software. I've spent 15+ years architecting Salesforce/HubSpot ecosystems and automating lead lifecycle workflows, but what excites me about Liatrio is the focus on LLMs and AI agents orchestrating the prospecting-to-routing pipeline - that's the next evolution of RevOps, not just optimization of legacy processes.

title: Growth Marketing Lead
description: Haus, an incrementality platform trusted by leading brands to optimize billions in ad spend, seeks a Growth Marketing Lead. The company uses causal inference-based econometric models to help brands measure the business impact of marketing, pricing, and promotions. Founded by former product managers, economists, and engineers from Google, Netflix, Meta, and Amazon, Haus makes decision science and causal marketing mix modeling accessible to businesses of all sizes. The role focuses on driving growth strategy and execution for this fast-scaling marketing analytics SaaS company.
score_reason: Strong company fit (AI-native GTM, B2B SaaS, decision science focus) and remote location work well, but Growth Marketing Lead is a lateral/slight step down from Omri's senior RevOps/Head of Revenue level—better suited for a Head of Growth or Chief Revenue Officer track.
ai_opener: Haus is solving a critical blind spot in marketing ROI measurement with causal inference—exactly the kind of AI-native GTM infrastructure that modern revenue teams need. Given your 15+ years building RevOps and GTM engines at scale, you'd likely see immediately how econometric modeling should feed directly into pipeline forecasting and CAC/LTV optimization. Worth a conversation about how you might shape growth strategy at a company this technically differentiated.

title: Growth & Lifecycle Marketing Lead: Expansion
description: Prompt, a B2B SaaS platform modernizing rehab therapy businesses, seeks a Growth Marketing Lead to own expansion and services revenue end-to-end. You'll drive cross-sell, upsell, and services growth across the existing customer base by diagnosing where expansion is constrained, building and executing the plan, and delivering measurable results. The role requires partnering closely with Product, Sales, Customer Success, Account Management, RevOps, and Creative teams. Ideal for a strategic operator who can set strategy and roll up their sleeves to do hands-on execution.
score_reason: Strong GTM and B2B SaaS background aligns well with expansion strategy and cross-functional RevOps collaboration, but this is primarily a marketing-focused growth role rather than core RevOps/GTM operations leadership that matches Omri's sweet spot.
ai_opener: Omri, I noticed Prompt is scaling expansion revenue across their therapy platform—a motion that benefits enormously from RevOps rigor and pipeline visibility. Given your track record building GTM infrastructure in B2B SaaS, you'd likely see immediate opportunities to instrument their expansion funnel and operationalize the cross-sell/upsell motion alongside their Product and CS teams.

title: Revenue Operations Manager
description: VulnCheck, a cybersecurity SaaS company, seeks a Revenue Operations Manager. Requires 4+ years in Revenue Operations, Sales Operations, or GTM Systems roles within B2B SaaS, with hands-on Salesforce administration (object design, workflow automation, validation rules, reporting) or HubSpot experience (lifecycle stages, lead scoring, marketing-to-sales handoff). You'll build executive-level dashboards covering pipeline health, conversion rates, forecasting, bookings, and churn/expansion. Strong working knowledge of SaaS metrics (ARR, ACV, sales velocity, pipeline coverage, NRR) and experience integrating a broader GTM tech stack required.
score_reason: Exceptional match: 15+ years B2B SaaS experience exceeds 4+ year requirement, strong Salesforce/HubSpot admin background aligns perfectly with hands-on technical needs, deep expertise in pipeline forecasting, SaaS metrics (ARR/ACV/NRR), and GTM tech stack integration directly addresses all core responsibilities.
ai_opener: VulnCheck's focus on building executive visibility into pipeline health and SaaS metrics resonates strongly with my background scaling RevOps at [similar-stage SaaS companies]. With 15+ years architecting Salesforce/HubSpot systems and forecasting models that drive predictable revenue, I've seen how the right operational foundation transforms cybersecurity GTM—particularly around conversion velocity and expansion metrics that matter for your model.

title: Sales Operations Analyst
description: Samsara, a connected operations SaaS platform, is hiring a Sales Operations Analyst (remote, US). The role supports the sales organization with operational analysis, reporting, and process optimization across the GTM motion. Samsara notes that official communication about applications only comes from emails ending in @samsara.com or @us-greenhouse-mail.io, cautioning against recruitment scams. This is a full-time analyst role within a high-growth public SaaS company focused on driving sales efficiency and data-driven decision-making.
score_reason: While Samsara is a strong B2B SaaS company and the role aligns with RevOps domain expertise, this is an Analyst-level IC position that underutilizes Omri's 15+ years of senior executive experience and leadership capabilities. The seniority mismatch makes this a poor career fit despite the company quality.
ai_opener: I've followed Samsara's growth trajectory as a connected operations leader, and your sales ops function appears to be at an inflection point. Given my experience building RevOps orgs from 0→1 and scaling GTM operations across hypergrowth phases, I'd be interested in exploring whether there might be a Head of Sales Operations or RevOps leadership role that better leverages the strategic side of what you're building.

title: Revenue Operations Business Analyst
description: SmithRx, a venture-backed health-tech SaaS company disrupting pharmacy benefit management, seeks a Revenue Operations Business Analyst as the primary liaison between RevOps and stakeholders. Requires 3+ years as a Business Analyst within a Revenue or Sales Operations organization. You'll conduct deep-dive sessions with sales, finance, legal, and account management to define business needs, translate them into requirements and user stories, develop enablement and training materials, manage UAT plans, and own the central repository of RevOps process documentation. Jira experience and AI familiarity preferred.
score_reason: Senior executive profile overqualified for analyst-level role, but strong RevOps fundamentals, stakeholder management, and process documentation expertise align well. Health-tech/SaaS domain fit and remote location are ideal, though title/level mismatch and lack of explicit Jira/BA emphasis present downside risk.
ai_opener: I've built RevOps functions from ground up at scale, and SmithRx's mission to modernize PBM resonates deeply—especially the complexity of coordinating across sales, finance, legal, and CS. Your emphasis on translating business needs into repeatable processes and owning documentation reflects exactly how I've structured RevOps teams to scale. I'd explore whether a senior-level RevOps partnership could accelerate your GTM maturity beyond the analyst scope.
